ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ASPX webshell drop by w3wp.exe in Exchange virtual directories (post-ProxyLogon chain)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM post-exploit fingerprint: the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) writing an .aspx/.ashx/.asmx file into an OWA / ECP / aspnet_client / Exchange FrontEnd path. This is exactly how the ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/-26857/-26858/-27065) chain drops China Chopper / 'reGeorg-style' webshells (Opal, Pleased, etc.), and the same pattern recurs in Silk Typhoon's 2024-2025 IT-supply-chain campaigns against on-prem Exchange / SharePoint.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename dest as host | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | where NOT match(file_path,"(?i)\\\\Setup\\\\") | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM webshell drop on Exchange — IIS worker writing ASPX into OWA/ECP/aspnet_client
let ExchangeWebRoots = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Ecp\"
]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"                                  // IIS worker only
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
| where FolderPath has_any (ExchangeWebRoots)
| where InitiatingProcessParentFileName !in~ ("Microsoft.Exchange.Setup.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe")  // strip CU/SU installs
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon webshell drop — ASIM file events; w3wp.exe writing ASPX into Exchange paths
let ExchangeWebRoots = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Ecp\"
]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where ActingProcessName endswith @"\w3wp.exe"
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx"
| where TargetFilePath has_any (ExchangeWebRoots)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName,
          ActingProcessCommandLine, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName,
          TargetFileSHA256, EventType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM ASPX Webshell Dropped by IIS w3wp.exe in Exchange Path
id: 9e8f4a02-1f3c-4b21-9a01-silktyphoon-uc1
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) creating .aspx/.ashx/.asmx files in OWA / ECP / aspnet_client / FrontEnd HttpProxy paths.
    Canonical Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM, G0125) post-exploit signature following the ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/-26857/-26858/-27065) chain
    and the 2024-2025 on-prem Exchange supply-chain follow-on campaigns.
references:
    - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
    - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
    - attack.initial-access
    - attack.persistence
    - attack.t1190
    - attack.t1505.003
    - attack.g0125
logsource:
    category: file_event
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_proc:
        Image|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    selection_ext:
        TargetFilename|endswith:
            - '.aspx'
            - '.ashx'
            - '.asmx'
    selection_path:
        TargetFilename|contains:
            - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
            - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\'
            - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\'
            - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
            - '\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\'
            - '\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Ecp\'
    filter_setup:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\Microsoft.Exchange.Setup.exe'
            - '\setup.exe'
            - '\msiexec.exe'
    condition: (selection_proc and selection_ext and selection_path) and not filter_setup
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate Exchange Cumulative / Security Update installation
    - Custom in-house OWA / ECP modules deployed by IT (correlate with change tickets)
level: high
ucs.rationale: w3wp.exe should NEVER author its own .aspx/.ashx/.asmx in OWA/ECP virtual directories outside an Exchange CU/SU install — this exact file-write provenance is the post-ProxyLogon webshell drop fingerprint Microsoft and Volexity attributed to HAFNIUM in March 2021, and the same drop pattern recurred in the 2024-2025 Silk Typhoon IT-supply-chain campaign. Generic 'webshell' rules fire on any ASPX in
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon cloud pivot — service principal credential addition followed by Microsoft Graph mailbox/file access from a new IP
ucs.description: Hunts Silk Typhoon's 2024-2025 tradecraft of pivoting from compromised on-prem identity / IT-management software into the victim's Entra tenant by adding a secondary credential (password/cert) to an existing OAuth application or service principal, then using that service principal to call Microsoft Graph (mail / files / users) from a never-before-seen IP. Microsoft's March 2025 Silk Typhoon advisory describes exactly this 'add-secret-to-existing-SP-then-pull-mail-via-Graph' flow.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstCredAdd from datamodel=Change where Change.change_type=AAD AND Change.action=created AND (Change.object_category="ApplicationCertificateAndSecretManagement" OR Change.object="Add service principal credentials" OR Change.object="Update application Certificates and secrets management") by Change.user Change.src Change.object_id Change.result_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Change)` | rename object_id as ServicePrincipalId | join type=inner ServicePrincipalId [| tstats summariesonly=true count as GraphCalls dc(Authentication.src) as DistinctIPs values(Authentication.src) as IPs min(_time) as firstSpUse max(_time) as lastSpUse from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.signature="ServicePrincipalSignIn" AND Authentication.app="Microsoft Graph" AND Authentication.action=success by Authentication.user Authentication.user_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | rename user_id as ServicePrincipalId] | where firstSpUse >= firstCredAdd AND (firstSpUse - firstCredAdd) < 86400 | eval credAddToFirstUseSec=firstSpUse-firstCredAdd | table firstCredAdd user src ServicePrincipalId credAddToFirstUseSec GraphCalls DistinctIPs IPs
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon — credential added to an existing AAD app/SP, then SP immediately calls Microsoft Graph from a new IP
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
// 1. Credential additions to apps / service principals (Entra audit)
let CredAdds = CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where Application == "Office 365"
    | where ActionType in ("Add service principal credentials.",
                          "Update application \u2013 Certificates and secrets management ",
                          "Update application - Certificates and secrets management",
                          "Update service principal",
                          "Add service principal.")
    | extend Target = tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData)).Target[0].ID)
    | extend ActorUpn = AccountDisplayName
    | project CredAddTime = Timestamp, ActorUpn, ActorIp = IPAddress,
              ActorCountry = CountryCode, ActionType, TargetSpId = Target,
              RawEventData;
// 2. Service principal sign-ins to Graph from public IPs in the recent window
let SpGraphSignIns = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(RecentHours)
    | where TokenIssuerType == "AzureAD"
    | where ResourceId == "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000"   // Microsoft Graph
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | where isnotempty(ApplicationId)
    | project SpSignInTime = Timestamp, ApplicationId, Application,
              SpIp = IPAddress, SpCountry = Country, AccountObjectId;
// 3. Correlate: cred-add immediately followed (\u2264 24h) by SP Graph call, from an IP not seen using that SP in the prior 14 days
let HistoricSpIps = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp between (ago(LookbackDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where ResourceId == "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000"
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | summarize KnownIps = make_set(IPAddress) by ApplicationId;
CredAdds
| join kind=inner SpGraphSignIns on $left.TargetSpId == $right.ApplicationId
| where SpSignInTime between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + 24h)
| join kind=leftouter HistoricSpIps on ApplicationId
| extend NewIp = iif(isnull(KnownIps) or not(set_has_element(KnownIps, SpIp)), true, false)
| where NewIp == true
| project CredAddTime, SpSignInTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SpSignInTime, CredAddTime),
          ActorUpn, ActorIp, ActorCountry,
          ApplicationId, Application,
          SpIp, SpCountry, ActionType
| order by SpSignInTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon cloud pivot — Entra cred-add then SP Graph call from a new IP
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
let CredAdds = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OperationName in~ ("Add service principal credentials",
                                "Update application \u2013 Certificates and secrets management ",
                                "Update application - Certificates and secrets management",
                                "Update service principal",
                                "Add service principal")
    | where Result =~ "success"
    | extend ActorUpn = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
             ActorIp  = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress),
             TargetSpId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id),
             TargetSpName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
    | project CredAddTime = TimeGenerated, ActorUpn, ActorIp, OperationName,
              TargetSpId, TargetSpName;
let HistoricSpIps = union isfuzzy=true AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs, AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(LookbackDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where ResourceId == "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000"
    | where ResultType == 0
    | summarize KnownIps = make_set(IPAddress) by ServicePrincipalId = coalesce(ServicePrincipalId, AppId);
let SpGraphRecent = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentHours)
    | where ResourceId == "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000"
    | where ResultType == 0
    | project SpSignInTime = TimeGenerated, ServicePrincipalId, ServicePrincipalName,
              AppId, SpIp = IPAddress, Location;
CredAdds
| join kind=inner SpGraphRecent on $left.TargetSpId == $right.ServicePrincipalId
| where SpSignInTime between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + 24h)
| join kind=leftouter HistoricSpIps on ServicePrincipalId
| extend NewIp = iif(isnull(KnownIps) or not(set_has_element(KnownIps, SpIp)), true, false)
| where NewIp == true
| project CredAddTime, SpSignInTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SpSignInTime, CredAddTime),
          ActorUpn, ActorIp, OperationName,
          ServicePrincipalId, ServicePrincipalName, SpIp, Location
| order by SpSignInTime desc
ucs.rationale: Silk Typhoon's 2024-2025 evolution (per Microsoft Threat Intelligence's March 2025 advisory) is to compromise IT-management / PAM / RMM tooling and steal API keys, then use those credentials to add a secondary secret/cert to an EXISTING legitimate OAuth app in the victim's Entra tenant — flying under app-consent monitoring — and immediately use that SP against Microsoft Graph to harvest mail and O
